name: Pair

aliases.name: Pair
aliases.comment:
`left` & `right` as a value-pair

    openClosedCurlyBracePair=
      { left= '{', right= '}' }

    openCurly= .left openClosedCurlyBracePair
    closedCurly= .right openClosedCurlyBracePair

values.name: decode
values.comment:
A `Json.Decode.Decoder` for `Pair`s, encoded by `encodePair`
    
    """
    {
     \"left\": 1,
     \"right\": 11
    }
    """
    |>Decode.decodeString
        (Pair.decode Decode.int Decode.int)


> `Ok { left= 1, right= 11 }`
values.type: Json.Decode.Decoder left -> Json.Decode.Decoder right -> Json.Decode.Decoder (Pair.Pair left right)

values.name: encode
values.type: (left -> Json.Encode.Value) -> (right -> Json.Encode.Value) -> Pair.Pair left right -> Json.Encode.Value

values.name: mapLeft
values.comment:
map the `left` value in a `Pair`

    { left= 128522, right= "smile" }
    |>mapLeft Char.fromCode

> `{ left= '😊', right= "smile" }`

This is handy when using maps or folds etc.

    namedEmojiCodes=
      PairDict.empty
      |>PairDict.insert { left= 127824, right= "pear" }
      |>PairDict.insert { left= 127795, right= "tree" }
    namedEmojis=
      namedEmojiCodes
      |>PairDict.map (Pair.mapLeft String.fromInt)

> `PairDict.fromList [ ( '🍐', "pear" ), ( '🌳', "tree" ) ]`
values.type: (left -> resultLeft) -> Pair.Pair left right -> Pair.Pair resultLeft right

values.name: mapRight
values.comment:
map the `right` value in a `Pair`

    { left= "smile", right= 128522 }
    |>mapRight Char.fromCode

> `{ left= "smile", right= '😊' }`

This is handy when using maps or folds etc.

    namedEmojiCodes=
      PairDict.empty
      |>PairDict.insert { left= "pear", right= 127824 }
      |>PairDict.insert {  left= "tree", right= 127795 }
    namedEmojis=
      namedEmojiCodes
      |>PairDict.map (Pair.mapLeft String.fromInt)

> `PairDict.fromList [ ( "pear", '🍐' ), ( "tree", '🌳' ) ]`
values.type: (right -> resultRight) -> Pair.Pair left right -> Pair.Pair left resultRight

name: PairDict

unions.name: PairDict
unions.comment:
Want to look up value-pairs from the left or the right?

Like [assoc-list](https://github.com/pzp1997/assoc-list),
a `PairDict` allows for anything as `left` or `right` values except for functions and things that contain functions.

## Example: cased letters
    lowerUppercaseLetters=
      empty
      |>insert { left= 'a', right= 'A' }
      |>insert { left= 'b', right= 'B' }
      |>insert { left= 'c', right= 'C' }

    upperCase char=
      rightOf char lowerUppercaseLetters

values.name: decode
values.comment:
A `Json.Decode.Decoder` for `PairDict`s, encoded by `encodePair`.

The order of insertion is not reconstructed (see `equal`)

    """
    [
     {
      \"left\": 2,
      \"right\": 22
     },
     {
      \"left\": 1,
      \"right\": 11
     }
    ]
    """
    |>Decode.decodeString
        (decode Decode.int Decode.int)

> `Ok (fromList [ ( 1, 11 ), ( 2, 22 ) ])`
values.type: Json.Decode.Decoder left -> Json.Decode.Decoder right -> Json.Decode.Decoder (PairDict.PairDict left right)

values.name: empty
values.comment: A `PairDict` with no elements
values.type: PairDict.PairDict left right

values.name: encode
values.type: (left -> Json.Encode.Value) -> (right -> Json.Encode.Value) -> PairDict.PairDict left right -> Json.Encode.Value

values.name: equal
values.comment:
using (==) built-in equality is often not useful in the context of association-dicts.

Ignoring insertion order: do these 2 `PairDict`s have the same size and identical `Pair`s?

    letterCodes=
      fromList [ ( 'a', 0 ), ( 'b', 1 ) ]
    fancyCompetingLetterCodes=
      fromList [ ( 'b', 1 ), ( 'a', 0 ) ]
    
    equal
      letterCodes
      fancyCompetingLetterCodes
    --> True
values.type: PairDict.PairDict left right -> PairDict.PairDict left right -> Basics.Bool

values.name: fold
values.comment:
Reduce the left-right `Pair`s from most recently inserted
to least recently inserted.
A fold in the other direction doesn't exist, as association-`Dict`s should rarely rely on order (see `equal`)

    brackets=
      fromList
        [ ( '(', ')' )
        , ( '{', '}' )
        ]
    openingAndClosing=
      brackets
      |>fold
          (\{ left, right } acc->
            acc ++[ String.fromList [ left, right ] ]
          )
          []
> `[ "{}", "()" ]`
values.type: (Pair.Pair left right -> acc -> acc) -> acc -> PairDict.PairDict left right -> acc

values.name: fromDict
values.comment:
Create a `PairDict` from a association-`Dict`. `left` is the key, `right` is the value.
If multiple equal keys or values are present, the value **first** in the `Dict` is **prefered**.
    
    lowerToUpperLetters=
      AssocList.Dict.empty
      |>AssocList.Dict.insert 'a' 'A'
      |>AssocList.Dict.insert 'b' 'B'

    lowerUpperLetters= fromDict lowerToUpperLetters
values.type: AssocList.Dict left right -> PairDict.PairDict left right

values.name: fromList
values.type: List.List ( left, right ) -> PairDict.PairDict left right

values.name: insert
values.comment:
Put in a left-right-`Pair`.

If either **value** is already **present**, the `PairDict` is **unchanged**.

    empty
    |>insert { left= 'b', right= 'B' } --puts it in 
    |>insret { right= 'A', left= 'a' } --puts it in
    |>insert { left= 'b', right= 'C' } --ignored, the left value already exists
    |>insert { left= 'c', right= 'A' } --ignored, the right value already exists
    |>insert { left= 'c', right= 'C' } --puts it in
values.type: Pair.Pair left right -> PairDict.PairDict left right -> PairDict.PairDict left right

values.name: leftOf
values.comment:
`Just` the right value if the left value is present in the `PairDict`, else `Nothing`

    casedLetters=
      empty
      |>insert { left= 'a', right= 'A' }
      |>insert { left= 'b', right= 'B' }

    upperCase char=
      rightOf char casedLetters
values.type: right -> PairDict.PairDict left right -> Maybe.Maybe left

values.name: lefts
values.comment:
values on the left

    brackets=
      fromList [ ( '(', ')' ), ( '{', '}' ) ]
    opening= lefts brackets
values.type: PairDict.PairDict left right -> List.List left

values.name: map
values.comment:
map pairs

    digitNames=
      fromList
        [ ( 0, "zero" )
        , ( 1, "one" )
        ]
    mathSymbolNames=
      digitNames
      |>map (Pair.mapLeft String.fromInt)
      |>insert { left= "+", right= "plus" }
values.type: (Pair.Pair left right -> Pair.Pair resultLeft resultRight) -> PairDict.PairDict left right -> PairDict.PairDict resultLeft resultRight

values.name: removeLeft
values.comment:
remove left-right pair.
If **`left` does not exist**, the `PairDict` is **unchanged**

    empty
    |>insert "(" ")"
    |>removeLeft ")" --unchanged, ")" is not a left value
    |>removeLeft "(" --removes { left= "(", right= ")" }
    --empty
values.type: left -> PairDict.PairDict left right -> PairDict.PairDict left right

values.name: removeRight
values.comment:
remove left-right pair.
If `right` does not exist, the `PairDict` is unchanged

    empty
    |>insert { left= "(", right= ")" }
    |>removeRight "(" --unchanged, "(" is not a right value
    |>removeRight ")" --removes { left= "(", right= ")" }
    --empty
values.type: right -> PairDict.PairDict left right -> PairDict.PairDict left right

values.name: rightOf
values.comment:
`Just` the right value if the left value is present in the `PairDict`, else `Nothing`

    casedLetters=
      empty
      |>insert { left= 'a', right= 'A' }
      |>insert { left= 'b', right= 'B' }

    lowerCase char=
      leftOf char casedLetters
values.type: left -> PairDict.PairDict left right -> Maybe.Maybe right

values.name: rights
values.comment:
values on the right

    brackets=
      fromList [ ( '(', ')' ), ( '{', '}' ) ]
    closing= rights brackets
values.type: PairDict.PairDict left right -> List.List right

values.name: size
values.comment:
How many pairs there are.

    size empty
> `0`
    
    size
      (fromList
        (List.range 0 41 |>List.map (\i-> ( i, i )))
      )
> `42`
values.type: PairDict.PairDict left right -> Basics.Int

values.name: toDict
values.comment:
Convert to an association-`Dict`, which you can access only from the left

    casedLetters=
      fromList
        [ ( 'A', 'a' ), ( 'B', 'b' ) ]
    lowerFromUpper=
      casedLetters |>dictFromLeft
values.type: PairDict.PairDict left right -> AssocList.Dict left right

values.name: union
values.comment:
Merge 2 `PairDict`s.
If a value on the left or right is present, prefer the second dict.

    numberNamedOperators=
      fromList
        [ ( '+', "plus" )
        , ( '-', "minus" )
        ]
    customNamedOperators=
      fromList
        [ ( '∧', "and" )
        , ( '∨', "or" )
        , ( '-', "negate" )
        ]
    validNamedOperators=
      union
        custumNamedOperators --has a '-' left
        numberOperatorNames --preferred → its '-'-Pair is inserted
values.type: PairDict.PairDict left right -> PairDict.PairDict left right -> PairDict.PairDict left right
